title: senecillis retusa
intro: Senecillis retusa, also known as groundsel, is a biennial plant that belongs to the daisy family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's leaves and flowers contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have antibacterial and antitumor properties, offering potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and fighting cancer. Senecillis retusa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with the tea being a popular remedy for coughs and colds.
